---
name: Good first issue
about: Self-contained, well-scoped work suitable for a first-time contributor
title: "[good-first-issue] "
labels: ["good first issue", "help wanted"]
assignees: []
---

> Step 97 — template for tagging contributor-friendly issues so the GitHub "good first issues" tab on the repo page is populated.

## The job

A short, plain-language description of what should change. **Be specific** about success criteria — "make it faster" is not a good-first-issue. "Add a skeleton loader to the alerts list while the API call is in flight" is.

## Where to look

- Primary file(s): `path/to/file.dart` lines NN–MM
- Related: `path/to/other_file.py`
- Tests: `flutter_app/test/foo_test.dart` or `tests/test_bar.py`

## What "done" looks like

- [ ] Concrete change 1
- [ ] Concrete change 2
- [ ] One test covering the new behaviour
- [ ] `pre-commit run --all-files` is green
- [ ] CI is green on the PR

## Useful reading first

- `CONTRIBUTING.md` for the local-setup walkthrough.
- `markdown-files/adr/ADR-001-mobile-shell.md` if the task is mobile.
- `markdown-files/technical/SSE_Contract.md` if the task touches the realtime stream.

## Hints

A short paragraph saying which approach we'd accept and which one to avoid. Saves a contributor from going down the wrong path before the first review.

## Mentor

@username — feel free to ping them in the PR for review or to ask early-stage questions.
